Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
George Frederick Wells papers
Wells, George Frederick.
2141
8 Linear Feet
1900-1958. Diaries, scrapbooks, manuscripts, printed materials, and correspondence. Papers of George Frederick Wells include diaries; writings on various theological questions; sermons; scrapbooks and notebooks; pamphlets, correspondence, tracts and church programs. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 45 - folder 3 45 - folder 2 45 - folder 1 ======= 45 - folder 3 45 - folder 2 45 - folder 1 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2088713
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, 2011
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, April 2016
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Methodist minister.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Papers of George Frederick Wells include diaries, 1905-1958; writings on the rural church and various theological questions; sermons; scrapbooks and notebooks; and pamphlets, tracts and church programs. Also, correspondence, 1900-1958, much of it with World War II servicemen, but also concerning the Country Church Movement.
Cite As:
George Frederick Wells papers, #2141. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Greene County (N.Y.) Dutchess County (N.Y.) Delaware County (N.Y.) Columbia County (N.Y.) Massachusetts. Vermont. World War, 1939-1945 -- Methodists. World War, 1939-1945 -- Paricipation, American. Religion -- Study and teaching -- United States. Methodist preaching. Methodist Church (U.S.) Methodists -- United States. Rural churches.

These papers were added to the folder and are not Well's. Church programs, book and magazine mail advertising, newspaper clippings, mostly columns by George Mathew Adams, Church pamphlets and tracts, two Pamphlets on "Atlantic Union", two pledge cards, calling card "Reverend Lincoln MacKenzie", membership card for G. F. W. for League of Vermont Writer, Valentine, Facsimile title page of Zion's Herald of 1823/01/09 - "The Federal Churches of Lincoln, Vermont. Articles of Amalgamation", reprint: "The Last Best Hope of Earth: An Interview with the Vice-President of the United State" by Frank S. Mead, Editor of the 'Christian Herald', "Appeal to Men in Service" by W. C. T. U., "The Ten Commandments of Safety", "Why I Go To Temple" by Samuel B. Finkel, and various scraps.BusinessUndated13
